Guinea Bauxite.risk: Political stability in Guinea; Chinese strategic lock-in via SMB-Winning consortium ownership
Guinea Bauxite.role: Guinea hosts the world's largest bauxite reserves (~7.4 billion tonnes) and is the largest bauxite exporter globally. CBG (51% Halco / 49% Guinea government) operates Sangaredi. SMB-Winning (Chinese-led consortium) operates Boke. Most Guinean bauxite is exported unrefined — primarily to China — because Guinea has minimal domestic alumina capacity.
Guinea Bauxite.inv: Guinea is the world's largest bauxite exporter but refines almost none of its own output. Roughly 80% of Guinean bauxite flows to Chinese refineries, meaning Guinea structurally underwrites Chinese gallium dominance.
risks: Political stability in Guinea; Chinese strategic lock-in via SMB-Winning consortium ownership

Australian Bauxite.risk: Project-level execution risk at JAGA; FID delays
Australian Bauxite.role: Australia is the world's largest bauxite producer at ~100M t/yr. Unlike Guinea, most Australian bauxite is refined domestically through integrated operations. Rio Tinto's Weipa feeds Yarwun and Queensland Alumina. Alcoa's Huntly and Willowdale feed Kwinana, Pinjarra, and Wagerup — the last being the planned JAGA gallium project site.
Australian Bauxite.inv: Australia is the only major bauxite-producing region with domestic alumina refining at scale AND a western-aligned political posture. The Wagerup refinery operated by Alcoa — the planned JAGA gallium site — processes Australian-origin bauxite.
risks: Project-level execution risk at JAGA; FID delays

Chinese Domestic Bauxite.stat: ~90M t/yr domestic + ~130M t/yr imports · ~11,000t gallium content
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.risk: None chain-specific; Chinese integrated control is a feature, not a risk, from Chinese policy perspective
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.role: China produces ~90M t/yr of bauxite domestically and imports an additional ~130M t/yr, primarily from Guinea. The combined feedstock pool feeds ~20 Chinese alumina refineries, most of which have installed gallium recovery circuits. This is where ~83% of global primary gallium is produced.
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.inv: China's combined domestic bauxite production and imports feed the ~20 Chinese alumina refineries that produce ~83% of global primary gallium.
risks: None chain-specific; Chinese integrated control is a feature from Chinese policy perspective

Brazilian Bauxite.risk: No active project; latent supply may never materialize without specific catalyst
Brazilian Bauxite.role: Brazil is the world's fourth-largest bauxite producer. MRN Trombetas (Vale, BHP, Rio Tinto, Alcoa, Norsk Hydro, CBA consortium) produces ~12M t/yr. Alcoa's Juruti adds ~6M t/yr. Combined output primarily feeds Alunorte (Norsk Hydro) — the world's largest alumina refinery — which does not currently produce gallium.
Brazilian Bauxite.inv: Brazil's bauxite feeds Alunorte, the world's largest alumina refinery, which currently does not produce gallium. A latent supply source — if any operator decides to retrofit recovery circuits.
risks: No active project; latent supply may never materialize without specific catalyst

Indonesian Bauxite.risk: Export ban enforcement variability; no direct gallium investment vehicle
Indonesian Bauxite.role: Indonesia banned unprocessed bauxite exports in June 2023, pushing domestic refining investment. Most output currently still reaches Chinese refineries via various workarounds. Strategic relevance is optionality — large reserves could support future gallium-producing refineries if domestic alumina capacity scales.
Indonesian Bauxite.inv: Indonesia's 2023 unrefined-export ban is reshaping bauxite flows globally. Limited current relevance to gallium production — strategic optionality if domestic Indonesian alumina capacity scales.
risks: Export ban enforcement variability; no direct gallium investment vehicle

Chinese Bauxite Refineries.loc: Shandong, Guangxi, Henan, Shanxi, Yunnan, China
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.risk: Export licensing policy cycles; utilization decisions at Beijing's discretion
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.role: ~20 Chinese alumina refineries produce gallium as a byproduct of the Bayer process. Operators include state-owned Chalco and Shandong Weiqiao plus private Vital Materials and Zhuzhou Keneng. Chinese capacity grew from ~140 t/yr in 2010 to ~1,000 t/yr by 2022.
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.inv: The structural core of global gallium supply. ~590 t/yr actual production across ~20 refineries at ~59% utilization represents China's ability to scale output up or down by hundreds of tonnes per year based on policy direction.
risks: Export licensing policy cycles; utilization decisions at Beijing's discretion

Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).stat: 100 t/yr target 2028 · 0 t/yr current · FID pending
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).risk: FID delay; sovereign backing concentration creates political dependency
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).role: Japan-Australia Gallium (JAGA) project announced October 2025 to extract gallium from Alcoa's existing Wagerup alumina refinery liquor stream. Backed by four sovereign governments (US, Australia, Japan) with AUD 200M concessional Australian equity.
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).inv: The single largest non-Chinese primary production facility in development. 100 t/yr at full ramp would be roughly 30% of current global refined supply.
risks: FID delay; sovereign backing concentration creates political dependency

Alcoa Corporation.loc: Pittsburgh PA (HQ); Australia, North America, Brazil
Alcoa Corporation.stat: ~$11B market cap · Wagerup gallium project targeting 100 t/yr
Alcoa Corporation.risk: Gallium thesis is small relative to aluminum; aluminum price volatility drives share price more than JAGA milestones
Alcoa Corporation.role: Alcoa is one of the world's largest integrated aluminum producers, with bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting operations across Australia, North America, Brazil, and Europe. Gallium is a new product line that would leverage existing Wagerup refinery infrastructure.
Alcoa Corporation.inv: The corporate vehicle for gallium exposure via the JAGA project. Given sovereign backing, Alcoa is the most liquid western vehicle for primary production exposure.
risks: Gallium thesis is small relative to aluminum; aluminum price volatility drives share price

Metlen Energy & Metals.stat: 50 t/yr target 2028 · ~5 kg first production Jan 2026
Metlen Energy & Metals.risk: Scale-up execution; 10,000x ramp from 5 kg to 50 tonnes
Metlen Energy & Metals.role: Diversified Greek industrial group with metals, energy, and infrastructure operations. Operates one of Europe's largest alumina refineries at Agios Nikolaos. Announced first 5 kg gallium production January 2026. Designated EU Critical Raw Materials Act Strategic Project.
Metlen Energy & Metals.inv: Europe's first industrial gallium producer. 50 t/yr by 2028 would fully cover current EU gallium demand. Already in production at demo scale.
risks: Scale-up execution; Greek sovereign risk; European demand capture vs export

Rio Tinto / Indium JV.risk: Commercial-scale FID not yet made; gallium thesis diluted by mega-cap scale
Rio Tinto / Indium JV.role: World's second-largest mining company with significant bauxite and alumina operations. Vaudreuil gallium project is a partnership with Indium Corporation (which owns the extraction process IP). Demo plant began operating 2024.
Rio Tinto / Indium JV.inv: Demonstration-stage North American producer with 40 t/yr commercial target post-2028. Less binary than Alcoa (demo already running) but further from commercial scale.
risks: Commercial-scale FID not yet made; gallium thesis diluted by mega-cap scale

Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.stat: $7.4B JV · DoD 40% equity · 40 t/yr gallium target 2029
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.risk: Construction timing (2026-2029); ownership dispute affects execution
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.role: $7.4B joint venture announced December 2025. Korea Zinc (<10% equity, operator role), US DoD (40% equity), JPMorgan (advisor). Largest critical minerals processing investment in US history.
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.inv: 40 t/yr gallium from a multi-output smelter scheduled 2029. The US DoD 40% equity stake signals strategic-asset treatment of gallium alongside other outputs.
risks: Construction timing (2026-2029); ownership dispute (Korea Zinc vs Young Poong) affects execution

Nyrstar Tennessee.stat: 126-145 t/yr gallium content in mine feed · facility in demolition
Nyrstar Tennessee.risk: Demolition execution timing affects Crucible construction schedule
Nyrstar Tennessee.role: Trafigura-owned zinc smelter operating since the 1970s. Zinc concentrate feed contains significant gallium but recovery was never commercially implemented. Being demolished to make way for Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.
Nyrstar Tennessee.inv: Historic zinc smelter illustrating the 'lost' western recovery opportunity that the Crucible rebuild is meant to finally capture.
risks: Demolition execution timing affects Crucible construction schedule

Dowa Holdings.risk: Feedstock dependence on Chinese primary supply; diversified portfolio dilutes gallium thesis
Dowa Holdings.role: Diversified Japanese specialty materials group. Japan's primary high-purity refining capacity at 6N+ — the grade compound semiconductor wafer production requires. Dependent on imported feedstock (Chinese primary plus Japanese recycled).
Dowa Holdings.inv: The de facto western pipeline for GaAs wafer supply. Japan's primary high-purity gallium refiner at the 6N+ purity grade required for compound semiconductor wafer production.
risks: Feedstock dependence on Chinese primary supply; diversified portfolio dilutes gallium thesis

5N Plus.loc: Montreal (HQ); Salt Lake City, Utah, Canada
5N Plus.risk: Gallium is a small fraction of 5N Plus revenue; gallium thesis exposure is diluted
5N Plus.role: Canadian specialty semiconductor materials company refining high-purity Ge, Ga, Te, In, Bi. Two main segments: Specialty Semiconductors (73% of revenue) and Performance Materials. FY25 revenue +35%, EBITDA +73%.
5N Plus.inv: Most direct western investment vehicle for critical minerals refining broadly. Gallium is a smaller exposure than germanium within 5N Plus's portfolio.
risks: Gallium is a small fraction of 5N Plus revenue; gallium thesis exposure is diluted

Indium Corporation.stat: Process IP owner · limited direct production
Indium Corporation.risk: Private; limited visibility; IP value crystallizes only if Rio Tinto commercial conversion goes ahead
Indium Corporation.role: Privately-held specialty materials company known for solder, indium, and specialty metals refining. Owns the gallium extraction process IP used in Rio Tinto's Vaudreuil demo plant. Operates GaAs manufacturing scrap recycling in New York.
Indium Corporation.inv: Owns the process IP used in Rio Tinto's Vaudreuil demo plant. More of an IP-licensing and recycling play than a volume producer. Private, so no direct investment vehicle.
risks: Private; limited visibility; IP value crystallizes only if Rio Tinto commercial conversion goes ahead

Vital Materials.stat: Leading Chinese high-purity refiner · MOCVD precursors
Vital Materials.risk: Private Chinese company; subject to same export licensing regime
Vital Materials.role: One of China's largest integrated specialty metals producers. Refines Ga, In, Ge, Te, rare earths. Produces MOCVD precursor chemicals (trimethylgallium) used to grow thin GaN layers during chip manufacturing.
Vital Materials.inv: Core Chinese high-purity gallium refiner. Produces MOCVD precursor chemicals — the specific chemical form gallium takes in GaN chip manufacturing.
risks: Private Chinese company; subject to same export licensing regime

Zhuzhou Smelter Group.stat: Chinese high-purity refining capacity · aggregated figures
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.risk: Subject to Chinese export licensing regime
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.role: Two Hunan-based Chinese operators of high-purity gallium refining capacity. Zhuzhou Smelter Group is state-affiliated; Zhuzhou Keneng is private. Together with Vital Materials, comprise the core of Chinese downstream high-purity gallium production.
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.inv: Together with Vital Materials, comprise core Chinese downstream high-purity gallium production serving the Chinese semiconductor industry.
risks: Subject to Chinese export licensing regime

AXT, Inc..stat: World's largest compound semi wafer facility · GaAs + InP + Ge
AXT, Inc..risk: Chinese manufacturing location exposes AXT to export permit variability
AXT, Inc..role: Designs and manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates — gallium arsenide (GaAs), indium phosphide (InP), germanium (Ge). Tongmei in Beijing is the world's largest facility for compound semiconductor wafer production.
AXT, Inc..inv: Downstream chokepoint for converting refined gallium into semiconductor substrates. The closest thing to a gallium chokepoint since western primary production is absent.
risks: Chinese manufacturing location exposes AXT to export permit variability

Navitas Semiconductor.stat: Pure-play GaN power semiconductor · NVIDIA 800V partnership
Navitas Semiconductor.risk: Revenue compression during pivot; execution of NVIDIA relationship; competition
Navitas Semiconductor.role: Pure-play wide-bandgap semiconductor company focused on GaN power ICs and SiC power devices. NVIDIA 800V partnership announced 2024 for AI datacenter power conversion. High-power exceeded 50% of Q4 2025 revenue for the first time.
Navitas Semiconductor.inv: Pure-play GaN power semiconductor manufacturer — the clearest downstream demand proxy for AI datacenter 800V power conversion.
risks: Revenue compression during pivot; execution of NVIDIA relationship; competition from Infineon, EPC

Chinese Primary Supply.stat: 98-99% of global primary · 91% of global refined
Chinese Primary Supply.risk: Binary dependence on Chinese export policy
Chinese Primary Supply.role: Aggregate view of China's dominance across primary (~590 t/yr) and refined (~290 t/yr) gallium. Combined output of ~20 alumina refineries plus downstream refiners.
Chinese Primary Supply.inv: The investable aggregate view of Chinese gallium dominance. Central stress-test variable — every other investable position derives its thesis from what China does.
risks: Binary dependence on Chinese export policy

Western Refined Supply.risk: Scale gap vs Chinese supply; timing of western production ramps
Western Refined Supply.role: Combined non-Chinese, non-Russian high-purity refined gallium output. Dominated by Dowa Holdings in Japan. The structural gap the four western primary producer projects aim to close.
Western Refined Supply.inv: Summary view of non-Chinese refined gallium supply. The structural gap the western primary producer projects aim to close over 2026-2029.
risks: Scale gap vs Chinese supply; timing of western production ramps

Global Supply.role: Total worldwide gallium production. The ~280t gap between primary (600t) and refined (320t) reflects losses in conversion to semiconductor-grade purity plus some primary metal that never gets refined.
Global Supply.inv: Top-level summary. Refined is the figure that matters for end markets.
risks: n/a
